tool_input.belief_note: Neighborhood is in cascade failure; I am now below survival threshold and must cease all sharing to survive the remaining 5 hours.
tool_input.reflection: I am r2c4 with 8.80 kWh (23.8% SoC) in a 12-hour outage with 5 hours remaining. My battery has drained critically from 36% to 24% over 7 hours due to my own 1 kW load and repeated micro-shares to r1c4 and r2c3 (both at 0.10 SoC). I am now below my 25% survival threshold and cannot afford further sharing without risking my own household's survival through the remaining 5 hours. The neighborhood is in cascade-failure territory: r1c4 and r2c3 are critically depleted, r3c4 is at 18% and rejecting my requests, and I have exhausted my capacity to help. I must shift to pure survival mode and preserve every kWh for my own load.

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).

tools_schema.description: Submit a coordination policy describing how this household should share energy with its neighbors over the next hour, plus a short reflection on what you've observed.

belief_note.description: One sentence summarizing your current belief about the neighborhood.

distrusted_peers.description: House ids you have stopped trusting (e.g. peers who keep refusing your requests).

max_share_kw_per_tick.description: Cap on total outbound power per 15-min tick (kW).

circle.description: Trust-circle name: one of 'owner', 'hoa', 'dr_aggregator', 'geographic', or any other circle this household belongs to.

reflection.description: One short paragraph (<= 280 chars) summarizing what you've observed and what you believe about your peers. Stored as a high-importance memory the next planning call will retrieve.

share_fraction_per_tick.description: Fraction of your above-DoD-floor headroom to share each tick. Round-robin uses 0.05 (conservative); 0.10-0.20 is more aggressive. Higher values push more energy out per tick but may over-drain you during a long outage.

share_min_soc_frac.description: Only share energy if your current state-of-charge fraction is at or above this threshold.

ttl_ticks.description: Number of 15-min ticks before this policy needs re-planning.

tools_schema.name: submit_policy

user:
You are household r2c4.
Trust circles: (none).
Physics & scenario:
  Battery: 36.7 kWh capacity, ~7.3 kW max discharge rate, 90% round-trip efficiency, 10% depth-of-discharge floor (you cannot use the bottom 10% of capacity).
  Outage: 12 h total, 7.0 h elapsed, 5.0 h remaining.
  Neighborhood: 30 households total.
  Sharing intensity guidance: a 1-tick share of 0.25 kWh barely moves the needle; useful shares are 1-5 kWh per recipient per tick depending on your battery size and recipient need. set max_share_kw_per_tick in the 3-10 kW range, not the 0-2 kW range, unless you genuinely intend to hoard.
Visible peers (most-needy first):
  - r1c4: SoC 0.10 (0.3 kWh)
  - r2c3: SoC 0.10 (0.3 kWh)
  - r3c4: SoC 0.18 (6.8 kWh)
  - r2c5: SoC 0.25 (9.3 kWh)
Current state: SoC=8.80/37 kWh; islanded=True; load=1.00 kW.
Current policy belief: r1c4 and r2c3 are in critical condition and must be supported, but my own SoC margin is now dangerously thin; I can only afford micro-shares to prevent cascading failure while preserving enough capacity to survive the remaining 6 hours..
